prevalence.source: Hematology epidemiology syntheses (AIHA and hereditary hemolytic anemias)
prevalence.trend: stable; detection improving with better lab access

burden.dalyPerCapita: low overall; can be severe in acute hemolysis or chronic transfusion needs
burden.mortalityRate: Low to moderate depending on cause and comorbidities
burden.source: IHME anemia and hematologic disease context

vulnerablePopulations: patients with autoimmune disease or malignancy
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: CBC plus hemolysis markers (LDH, bilirubin, haptoglobin), smear, DAT/Coombs for immune hemolysis; evaluate underlying etiology

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; evaluated when symptomatic or abnormal CBC found
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; improves with timely diagnosis and targeted therapy

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: DAT testing availability varies; advanced work-up concentrated in tertiary centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Treat underlying cause; steroids for warm AIHA, immunosuppressants/rituximab when indicated; transfusions; folate supplementation
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Specialist access and high cost of advanced immunotherapies for some patients
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Core diagnostics and steroid-based treatment widely available
